trackingsummarydata.subject: This week in A&S: Cornell Cinema's season, Russian forests and research opps
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG - 01/26/26

urls.page_title: Cornell Cinema unveils diverse, collaboration-driven spring lineup
urls.page_summary: From midcentury melodramas to speculative visions of technology and the human body—and even a French coming of age story about crafting world class cheese—Cornell Cinema’s spring season offers a varied plate.
urls.page_title: Bruce V. Lewenstein | Department of Science & Technology Studies
Why did Jeffrey Epstein cultivate famous scientists? | Scientific American
urls.page_summary: The Epstein files revive questions of whether the disgraced financier sought to merely cultivate famous scientists, or to shape science itself
urls.page_title: ICE protesters on a tightrope between conflict and peace
urls.page_summary: Minnesota officials have urged protesters to stay peaceful and monitor ICE, but doing so has brought backlash from the Trump administration.
urls.page_title: Spring 2026 Latina/o Studies Fridays with Faculty seminar series | Latina/o Studies Program
urls.page_summary: The Latina/o Studies Fridays with Faculty luncheon seminar offers an opportunity for students of all levels and disciplines to meet faculty and administrators from across the university for informal conversation about their current research/work i...
urls.page_title: Recounting Russia’s history through its forests
urls.page_summary: The fate of Russia’s forests will affect the whole world, according to a new book by a Cornell researcher.

The Humanities Scholars Program fosters independent, interdisciplinary undergraduate research in the humanities, and provides a supportive community, through a series of curated courses, structured mentorship, special programming, and research opp...
urls.page_title: Trade expert discusses misconceptions about trade Jan. 29
urls.page_summary: Scott Lincicome will talk about imports, tariffs and globalization during his talk, “What You Think You Know About Trade is Probably Wrong."
